docs.title: SOC Detection Requirements
docs.text:
Welcome to the Blue Team Detection Requirements documentation site.

This site is the single source of truth for all detection engineering requirements tracked by the Security Operations Centre (SOC). Every detection is version-controlled in the repository and published here automatically on each merge to main.

docs.title: 🎯 What Is This Site?
docs.text:
Each page in this site corresponds to a detection requirement — a structured document that captures:

  • What behaviour we want to detect
  • Why it matters (threat scenario and MITRE ATT&CK mapping)
  • How to detect it (data sources and query logic)
  • Current status of the detection (Planned → In Testing → Provisioned)

docs.title: Detection Grouping
docs.text:
This site is intentionally grouped by adversary behavior, not by platform silo.

Grouping Description Threat Actors Coverage by intrusion sets/groups mapped from live MITRE ATT&CK data Tactics Coverage by MITRE ATT&CK tactic and attack phase Live ATT&CK Mapping Technique-to-group/software/detection mapping with refresh controls

Use actor pages to answer "what do we detect for this adversary?" and tactic pages to answer "which attack phases are covered or missing?"

docs.title: 📊 Detection Status Legend
docs.text: Badge Meaning 🟢 Provisioned Detection is active and tuned in the SIEM / EDR 🟡 In Testing Detection deployed but undergoing validation and tuning 🔵 Planned Requirement identified; detection not yet built

docs.title: ➕ Adding a New Detection
docs.text:
1. Copy templates/detection-template.md from the repository.
  2. Create a new file in detections/<category>/DET-X-NNN.md.
  3. Fill in all fields, including threat actor and ATT&CK tactic metadata.
  4. Ensure frontmatter includes ATT&CK IDs (mitre_tactics, mitre_techniques) and actor tags.
  5. Run the Refresh MITRE ATT&CK Data workflow to refresh group/software mappings from MITRE.
  6. Open a Pull Request — the site rebuilds automatically on merge.

See the project README for full contribution guidelines.

docs.title: Threat Actors
docs.text: This section is now sourced from live MITRE ATT&CK data and local detection metadata.

docs.title: How Actor Mapping Works
docs.text:
• Techniques in local detections are mapped to MITRE ATT&CK Groups and Software
  • Group and software relationships are read from the latest MITRE STIX feed
  • Results are published to docs/data/mitre_live_mapping.json

docs.title: View Live Actor Coverage
docs.text:
• Open Live ATT&CK Mapping
  • In the Groups Coverage table, each group shows:
  • mapped techniques
  • mapped local detections

docs.title: Refresh from MITRE
docs.text:
Use the workflow run button to refresh repository data:

  • Refresh MITRE ATT&CK Data

After running the workflow, the site will include updated group and software mappings based on current MITRE content.

docs.title: Cloud Detections
docs.text:
Cloud platform detection requirements covering AWS, Azure, and GCP.

Detection ID Title Status DET-C-001 AWS S3 Bucket Made Public 🟢 Provisioned

docs.title: [DET-C-001] AWS S3 Bucket Made Public
docs.text: Field Value Detection ID DET-C-001 Category Cloud Status 🟢 Provisioned MITRE Technique T1567 – Exfiltration Over Web Service

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Detects when an AWS S3 bucket ACL or bucket policy is modified to allow public access, which may indicate a misconfiguration or intentional data exposure by an insider threat or compromised credential.

docs.title: Use Case
docs.text: Public S3 bucket exposure is one of the most common causes of cloud data breaches. Monitoring PutBucketAcl and PutBucketPolicy API calls that grant public access provides early warning.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Platform Notes AWS CloudTrail AWS Management events; s3.amazonaws.com event source AWS Config AWS Configuration change history for S3 resources

docs.title: Logic / Query
docs.text:
// Microsoft Sentinel – KQL (AWS CloudTrail via AWSCloudTrail table)
AWSCloudTrail
| where EventSource == "s3.amazonaws.com"
| where EventName in ("PutBucketAcl", "PutBucketPolicy")
| where RequestParameters has_any ("AllUsers", "AuthenticatedUsers", "\"Effect\":\"Allow\"")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    UserIdentityArn,
    SourceIPAddress,
    AWSRegion,
    RequestParameters,
    EventName,
    Resources

docs.title: Alert Thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Value Threshold Any single occurrence Severity Critical Confidence High

docs.title: Response Actions
docs.text:
1. Immediately re-apply block-public-access settings at the bucket and account level.
  2. Identify which IAM principal made the change and review recent API activity.
  3. Check CloudTrail GetObject logs to determine if data was accessed while public.
  4. Notify the data owner and initiate a data breach assessment if sensitive data was exposed.
  5. Rotate the compromised IAM credentials if an external actor is suspected.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• MITRE ATT&CK – T1567
  • AWS – Blocking public access to S3
  • Full detection file on GitHub

docs.title: Endpoint Detections
docs.text:
Host-based detection requirements covering Windows, Linux, and macOS endpoints.

Detection ID Title Status DET-E-001 Brute Force Attack – Local Windows Account 🟢 Provisioned DET-E-002 Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution 🟢 Provisioned

docs.title: [DET-E-001] Brute Force Attack – Local Windows Account
docs.text: Field Value Detection ID DET-E-001 Category Endpoint Status 🟢 Provisioned MITRE Technique T1110.001 – Brute Force: Password Guessing

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Detects repeated failed logon attempts against a local Windows account within a short time window, indicative of an online brute-force or password-spray attack against endpoints.

docs.title: Use Case
docs.text: Adversaries attempting to gain access to an endpoint may systematically try common passwords or user-name/password combinations. This detection fires when there are more than 10 failed interactive or network logons for a single account within a 5-minute window.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Platform Notes Windows Security Event Log Windows Endpoint Event ID 4625 (Failed Logon) Windows Security Event Log Windows Endpoint Event ID 4624 (Successful Logon – for correlation)

docs.title: Logic / Query
docs.text:
// Microsoft Sentinel – KQL
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4625
| where LogonType in (2, 3, 10)          // Interactive, Network, RemoteInteractive
| summarize
    FailedLogons = count(),
    DistinctIPs  = dcount(IpAddress)
    by Account, Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where FailedLogons > 10
| extend Severity = "Medium"

docs.title: Alert Thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Value Threshold > 10 failed logons within 5 minutes per account/host Severity Medium Confidence High

docs.title: Response Actions
docs.text:
1. Identify the source IP and check for geo-anomalies.
  2. Check for a successful logon (Event ID 4624) shortly after the failures.
  3. Isolate the endpoint if active compromise is suspected.
  4. Reset the targeted account password if brute force succeeded.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• MITRE ATT&CK – T1110.001
  • Microsoft – Event ID 4625
  • Full detection file on GitHub

docs.title: [DET-E-002] Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
docs.text: Field Value Detection ID DET-E-002 Category Endpoint Status 🟢 Provisioned MITRE Technique T1059.001 – PowerShell

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Detects PowerShell processes launched with -EncodedCommand (or its aliases) which is commonly used by threat actors to obfuscate malicious payloads and evade signature-based detection.

docs.title: Use Case
docs.text: Attackers frequently use Base64-encoded PowerShell commands to bypass command-line logging, deliver payloads, or establish persistence.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Platform Notes Sysmon Event ID 1 (Process Create) Windows Endpoint Requires Sysmon v13+ Windows Security Event ID 4688 Windows Endpoint Requires command-line auditing enabled Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Windows Endpoint DeviceProcessEvents table

docs.title: Logic / Query
docs.text:
// Microsoft Sentinel – KQL (Microsoft Defender for Endpoint)
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-e(nc(odedcommand)?)?\s+[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}"
| project
    Timestamp,
    DeviceName,
    InitiatingProcessAccountName,
    ProcessCommandLine,
    InitiatingProcessFileName,
    InitiatingProcessCommandLine

docs.title: Alert Thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Value Threshold Any occurrence (single-event trigger) Severity High Confidence Medium

docs.title: Response Actions
docs.text:
1. Decode the Base64 payload and analyse its content.
  2. Check for child processes spawned by the PowerShell instance.
  3. Review network connections established during or after execution.
  4. Quarantine the endpoint if malicious payload confirmed.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• MITRE ATT&CK – T1059.001
  • Microsoft – PowerShell logging
  • Full detection file on GitHub

docs.title: Identity Detections
docs.text:
Authentication, authorisation, and identity anomaly detection requirements.

Detection ID Title Status DET-I-001 Impossible Travel – Simultaneous Logons from Different Countries 🟢 Provisioned

docs.title: [DET-I-001] Impossible Travel
docs.text: Field Value Detection ID DET-I-001 Category Identity Status 🟢 Provisioned MITRE Technique T1078 – Valid Accounts

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Detects when the same user account authenticates successfully from two geographically distant locations within a timeframe that is physically impossible given normal travel constraints (< 2 hours apart, different countries).

docs.title: Use Case
docs.text: Account compromise can be identified by correlating successful logon events from IP addresses that resolve to different countries within a short window.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Platform Notes Azure AD Sign-In Logs Azure / Entra ID SigninLogs table in Log Analytics Okta System Log SaaS / IdP authentication.success events GeoIP enrichment Threat Intel MaxMind GeoLite2 or Azure built-in

docs.title: Logic / Query
docs.text:
// Microsoft Sentinel – KQL (Azure AD Sign-In Logs)
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0  // Successful logon
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| summarize
    Countries       = make_set(Country),
    IPs             = make_set(IPAddress),
    LogonCount      = count(),
    FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 2h)
| where array_length(Countries) > 1
| extend TimeDeltaHours = datetime_diff("hour", LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where TimeDeltaHours < 2

docs.title: Alert Thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Value Time window 2 hours Distance threshold Any two different countries Severity High Confidence High

docs.title: Response Actions
docs.text:
1. Contact the user via an out-of-band channel to verify the logons.
  2. Force a password reset and MFA re-registration if compromise suspected.
  3. Revoke all active sessions via Azure AD / Okta.
  4. Review recent activity for data exfiltration indicators.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• MITRE ATT&CK – T1078
  • Microsoft Sentinel – Built-in analytic rules
  • Full detection file on GitHub

docs.title: Network Detections
docs.text:
Network traffic and protocol-level detection requirements.

Detection ID Title Status DET-N-001 DNS Beaconing to Suspicious Domain 🟡 In Testing

docs.title: [DET-N-001] DNS Beaconing to Suspicious Domain
docs.text: Field Value Detection ID DET-N-001 Category Network Status 🟡 In Testing MITRE Technique T1071.004 – DNS

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Detects internal hosts making highly regular DNS queries to the same external domain at a consistent interval, characteristic of C2 beacon check-ins using DNS as the transport channel.

docs.title: Use Case
docs.text: Command-and-control frameworks use periodic DNS queries to maintain a covert channel. Regular beaconing produces a distinct time-series signature — high query frequency, low jitter, single target domain.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Data Source Platform Notes DNS Query Logs Infra / Network Recursive resolver or DNS firewall logs Zeek dns.log Network Sensor Fields: id.orig_h, query, rcode_name Azure DNS Analytics Cloud DNS query logs forwarded to Log Analytics

docs.title: Logic / Query
docs.text:
// Splunk SPL – DNS beaconing via consistent query intervals
index=dns sourcetype=zeek_dns
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats count as QueryCount, dc(query) as UniqDomains by src_ip, _time
| where QueryCount > 60 AND UniqDomains == 1
| eval BeaconRate = QueryCount / 60
| where BeaconRate >= 0.8
| table _time, src_ip, QueryCount, UniqDomains, BeaconRate

docs.title: Alert Thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Value Threshold ≥ 60 queries/hour to a single domain Severity High Confidence Medium

docs.title: Response Actions
docs.text:
1. Check domain reputation (VirusTotal, PassiveDNS).
  2. Block the domain at the DNS firewall and perimeter.
  3. Capture a full packet trace from the source host.
  4. Isolate the host if C2 communication is confirmed.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• MITRE ATT&CK – T1071.004
  • Zeek DNS log reference
  • Full detection file on GitHub

docs.title: MITRE ATT&CK Tactics
docs.text: This section is maintained from live ATT&CK content, not static tactic snapshots.

docs.title: Tactic-First Model
docs.text:
• A single detection may span multiple categories
  • Coverage is measured by ATT&CK behavior and phase
  • Technique, group, and software relationships come from MITRE STIX

docs.title: Live Tactic Coverage
docs.text:
Open Live ATT&CK Mapping to view:

  • tactics mapped to your local detections
  • techniques under each tactic
  • linked groups and software from MITRE

docs.text:
Run this workflow whenever you want the latest MITRE updates in-repo:
